title: Burnley Council spending rose 8.3% to £43.3M
summary: Burnley Borough Council's net current expenditure rose by £3.3million (8.3%) in 2024-25, according to GOV.UK data. Total spending reached £43.3million, compared to £40.0million in 2023-24.

title: Crime in Burnley: 1119 offences reported in 2025-12
summary: 1119 crimes were reported in Burnley during 2025-12. The most common offences were violent crime (364 cases), followed by anti-social behaviour (234 cases). Police resolved 79% of cases.

title: Sentence date set for man found guilty of sex offences against 8-year-old girl
summary: A man has been found guilty of three historic sexual offences against a girl in Burnley.
ai_rewrite: A sentencing date has been scheduled for a man found guilty of historic sexual offences against an eight-year-old girl in Burnley. The conviction follows legal proceedings regarding crimes committed against the child in the area.

title: How a magnate's fortune still funds Burnley's arts
summary: Edward Stocks Massey left his entire fortune in 1909 to support the arts and education in Burnley.
ai_rewrite: More than a century after his death, mill owner Edward Stocks Massey continues to shape Burnley's cultural landscape through a legacy established in 1909. His endowment still finances arts programmes and educational initiatives across the town today. The historic bequest ensures that Burnley residents continue to benefit from free cultural resources funded by the industrialist's generosity.

title: Environment Agency given £5m for Lancashire flood defences set to be completed by 2029
summary: The Environment Agency has been given £5 million to progress the building of flood defences in Padiham with a view to completing them in 2029.
ai_rewrite: The Environment Agency has secured £5 million funding to construct flood defences in Padiham. Work on the Lancashire town's protective barriers is expected to finish by 2029. The investment aims to safeguard local homes and businesses from future flooding events.

title: 'Venomous cobra' reported to council turns out to be old bucket | ITV News
summary: 'Venomous cobra' reported to council turns out to be old bucket | ITV News    ITVX
ai_rewrite: Lancashire council officers responded to reports of a dangerous snake in the area, only to find the supposed cobra was actually a discarded bucket. The mix-up required local authority resources to investigate the potential threat before the harmless item was identified. Officials have confirmed the incident concluded without risk to the public.

title: The one council that will hold elections in May
summary: The one council that will hold elections in May    BBC
ai_rewrite: Only one council will hold elections this May, and Lancashire residents should verify whether their district is affected by this unusual polling schedule. Voters in the relevant borough must register and prepare ballots while neighbouring authorities postpone electoral decisions. The isolated contest will test local party strength and determine representation for services and planning.

title: Rapist beat woman with metal bar and slashed her with knife in five-hour Burnley attack
summary: A man who subjected a woman to a prolonged violent rape attack in Burnley has been jailed.
ai_rewrite: A man has been imprisoned following a five-hour assault in Burnley where he attacked a woman with a metal bar and knife. The prolonged violent incident, which included rape, took place in the Lancashire town. The sentencing brings justice for the victim after the brutal attack.

title: Woman pushed from van and dragged along Preston street as police hunt wanted man who may be in Burnley
summary: A man is wanted by police after a woman was pushed from a van and dragged along a Preston street while holding onto the vehicle.
ai_rewrite: Police are searching for a suspect following a disturbing incident in Preston where a woman was pushed from a van and dragged along the road. The victim was holding onto the vehicle when she was forced out in the Lancashire city. Detectives believe the wanted man may have fled to Burnley, and are appealing for local information to locate him.
